---
title: "07b_Functional_Analysis_ORA (fixed)"
author: "automated-patch"
date: "2026-03-05"
output: pdf_document
---

# 1. Setup

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(here)
base_dir <- here::here("Proteomics")
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = base_dir)

# Ensure directories exist
results_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "results", "functional_analysis_ora")
dir.create(results_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

```{r libs, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
pkgs <- c("WGCNA", "dplyr", "readr", "clusterProfiler", "org.Hs.eg.db", 
          "enrichplot", "ReactomePA", "ggplot2", "openxlsx", "stringr")

# Install missing packages first
for(pk in pkgs){
  if(!requireNamespace(pk, quietly = TRUE)){
    message("Installing missing package: ", pk)
    if(pk %in% c("WGCNA", "clusterProfiler", "org.Hs.eg.db", "enrichplot", "ReactomePA")){
      if(!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
      BiocManager::install(pk, ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
    } else {
      install.packages(pk)
    }
  }
}

# Load packages after ensuring all are installed
lapply(pkgs, library, character.only = TRUE)
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

# 2. Load Data

```{r load-data}
# Load WGCNA outputs
wgcna_file <- file.path(base_dir, "results", "wgcna", "WGCNA2_core_outputs.RData")
if(!file.exists(wgcna_file)) stop("WGCNA output not found at: ", wgcna_file)
load(wgcna_file)

# Check loaded objects
if(!exists("expr") || !exists("moduleColors")) stop("WGCNA objects 'expr' or 'moduleColors' missing.")
stopifnot(colnames(expr) == names(moduleColors))

# Load Annotation
analyte_file <- file.path(base_dir, "data", "CSF_SOMAscan7k_analyte_information.tsv")
if(!file.exists(analyte_file)) stop("Analyte annotation not found: ", analyte_file)
analyte_info <- read.delim(analyte_file, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, check.names = FALSE)
```

# 3. ID Mapping (SOMAmer -> SYMBOL -> ENTREZ)

```{r id-mapping}
all_features <- colnames(expr)

feature_key <- analyte_info %>%
  dplyr::filter(Analytes %in% all_features) %>%
  dplyr::select(Analytes, EntrezGeneSymbol) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  dplyr::rename(SOMAmer = Analytes, SYMBOL = EntrezGeneSymbol)

# Map to Entrez
map_all <- bitr(
  feature_key$SYMBOL,
  fromType = "SYMBOL",
  toType   = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb    = org.Hs.eg.db
) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(SYMBOL, .keep_all = TRUE)

feature_map <- feature_key %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(map_all, by = "SYMBOL")

# Universe background
universe_entrez <- unique(feature_map$ENTREZID)
message("Mapped ", length(universe_entrez), " unique Entrez IDs from ", length(all_features), " SOMAmers.")
```

# 4. ORA Execution

```{r ora-functions}
modules_to_export <- setdiff(unique(moduleColors), "grey")

get_module_entrez <- function(mod) {
  somamers <- colnames(expr)[moduleColors == mod]
  feature_map %>%
    filter(SOMAmer %in% somamers) %>%
    pull(ENTREZID) %>%
    unique()
}

run_ORA_GO_BP <- function(entrez) {
  enrichGO(
    gene          = entrez,
    universe      = universe_entrez,
    OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
    keyType       = "ENTREZID",
    ont           = "BP",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    qvalueCutoff  = 0.1,
    readable      = TRUE
  )
}

run_ORA_KEGG <- function(entrez) {
  enrichKEGG(
    gene          = entrez,
    universe      = universe_entrez,
    organism      = "hsa",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    qvalueCutoff  = 0.1
  )
}

run_ORA_Reactome <- function(entrez) {
  enrichPathway(
    gene          = entrez,
    universe      = universe_entrez,
    organism      = "human",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    qvalueCutoff  = 0.1,
    readable      = TRUE
  )
}
```

```{r run-ora}
ora_results <- list(
  GO_BP   = list(),
  KEGG    = list(),
  Reactome = list()
)

for (mod in modules_to_export) {
  message("Running ORA for module: ", mod)
  entrez <- get_module_entrez(mod)
  if (length(entrez) < 10) {
    message("  -> Skipping, fewer than 10 mapped genes.")
    next
  }
  ora_results$GO_BP[[mod]]    <- run_ORA_GO_BP(entrez)
  ora_results$KEGG[[mod]]     <- run_ORA_KEGG(entrez)
  ora_results$Reactome[[mod]] <- run_ORA_Reactome(entrez)
}
```

# 5. Save Results

```{r save-ora-results}
save_ORA_results <- function(results_object, outdir, padj_cutoff = 0.05) {
  dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  for (db in names(results_object)) {
    wb <- createWorkbook()
    message("Creating workbook for: ", db)
    modules_list <- results_object[[db]]
    
    for (mod in names(modules_list)) {
      res <- modules_list[[mod]]
      if (is.null(res) || nrow(as.data.frame(res)) == 0) next

      df <- as.data.frame(res) %>% filter(p.adjust < padj_cutoff)
      if (nrow(df) == 0) next

      if ("geneID" %in% colnames(df)) {
        df$geneID <- sapply(strsplit(df$geneID, "/"), paste, collapse = ", ")
      }

      addWorksheet(wb, mod)
      writeData(wb, mod, df)
    }

    if (length(wb$worksheets) > 0) {
      file_path <- file.path(outdir, paste0("ORA_", db, "_Results.xlsx"))
      saveWorkbook(wb, file = file_path, overwrite = TRUE)
      message("  -> Saved: ", file_path)
    } else {
      message("  -> No significant pathways to save for ", db)
    }
  }
}

save_ORA_results(ora_results, results_dir)
```

# 6. Visualization

```{r plot-ora-dotplots}
plot_ORA_dotplot <- function(ora_object, module_name, database = "GO_BP", top_n = 15, padj_cutoff = 0.05) {
  res <- ora_object[[database]][[module_name]]
  if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)

  df <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
    filter(p.adjust < padj_cutoff) %>%
    arrange(p.adjust) %>%
    head(top_n)
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)

  df$GeneRatioNumeric <- sapply(df$GeneRatio, function(x) eval(parse(text = x)))
  df$Description <- str_wrap(df$Description, width = 50)

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = GeneRatioNumeric, y = reorder(Description, GeneRatioNumeric))) +
    geom_point(aes(size = Count, color = -log10(p.adjust))) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = paste(database, "Enrichment – Module", module_name),
      x = "Gene Ratio", y = NULL,
      color = "-log10(adj.p)", size = "Gene Count"
    )
  return(p)
}

# Save all dotplots
plots_dir <- file.path(results_dir, "plots")
dir.create(plots_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (db in names(ora_results)) {
  for (mod in names(ora_results[[db]])) {
    p <- plot_ORA_dotplot(ora_results, module_name = mod, database = db)
    if (!is.null(p)) {
      ggsave(
        filename = file.path(plots_dir, paste0(db, "_", mod, "_dotplot.png")),
        plot = p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300
      )
    }
  }
}
```

# 7. Hub Gene Extraction

```{r hub-gene-extraction}
# Recompute kME for hub gene analysis
datKME <- as.data.frame(cor(expr, MEs, use = "p"))
colnames(datKME) <- paste0("kME_", sub("^ME", "", colnames(MEs)))
rownames(datKME) <- colnames(expr)

extract_hub_genes_per_pathway <- function(module_name, database = "GO_BP", kME_threshold = 0.7, padj_cutoff = 0.05) {
  ora_res <- ora_results[[database]][[module_name]]
  if (is.null(ora_res)) return(NULL)

  df <- as.data.frame(ora_res) %>% filter(p.adjust < padj_cutoff)
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)

  kme_col <- paste0("kME_", module_name)
  module_genes <- feature_map %>%
    filter(SOMAmer %in% colnames(expr)[moduleColors == module_name]) %>%
    left_join(
      data.frame(SOMAmer = rownames(datKME), kME = datKME[, kme_col]),
      by = "SOMAmer"
    ) %>%
    filter(abs(kME) >= kME_threshold)

  pathway_list <- list()
  for (i in 1:nrow(df)) {
    pathway_name <- df$Description[i]
    pathway_genes <- unlist(strsplit(df$geneID[i], "/"))
    hubs <- module_genes %>%
      filter(SYMBOL %in% pathway_genes) %>%
      arrange(desc(kME))
    if (nrow(hubs) > 0) {
      pathway_list[[pathway_name]] <- hubs %>% select(SOMAmer, SYMBOL, ENTREZID, kME)
    }
  }
  return(pathway_list)
}

# Save hub genes to Excel
hub_dir <- file.path(results_dir, "HubGenes")
dir.create(hub_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (mod in modules_to_export) {
  hub_data <- extract_hub_genes_per_pathway(module_name = mod, database = "GO_BP")
  if (is.null(hub_data) || length(hub_data) == 0) next

  wb <- createWorkbook()
  for (pathway in names(hub_data)) {
    # Sanitize sheet name
    sheet_name <- substr(gsub("[:\\\\/?*\\[\\]]", "", pathway), 1, 31)
    addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
    writeData(wb, sheet_name, hub_data[[pathway]])
  }
  
  if(length(wb$worksheets) > 0) {
      saveWorkbook(
        wb,
        file = file.path(hub_dir, paste0("HubGenes_GO_BP_", mod, ".xlsx")),
        overwrite = TRUE
      )
  }
}
```
